name: Bell, Isaac Fulton
motto: "O Change, O Wondrous Change! Burst are the prison bars."; "Ennobling this dull pomp By contemplation of diviner things."
description: If you wish to know about any of the ancient happenings at Ole Miss ask Judge, he's a man for a' that.; Bell's splendid executive ability has made him respected by all.

name: Berry, Sidney B.
motto: "He'll walk evermore to higher paths Illumined by bright rays from Reason's shining lamp."
clubs: Chairman Gordon Hall Board of Control
description: A man born to wield the cudgel and instruct juries. "Sid's" only set-back was that his home was in Simpson County, but it is only fair to let it be known that he speedily overcame this.

name: Bieker, Siegfried B.
motto: "After death the doctor!"; "I love tranquil solitude And such society As is quiet, wise and good."
description: If "Bick" has the success with that girl that he has had with the Med. Course he'll be fixed "okeh."; "Beik" is just simply one of these "Genius will out" cases.

name: Wiginton, M. C.
motto: "But when it is indeed, Dismissing the doctor does not always succeed."; "Great thoughts, great feelings came to him, Like instincts, unawares."
hometown: Bara Avis, Miss.; Rara Avis, Miss.
description: Yes, they called him "Jazz Baby" but don't be deceived by that. He has mopped up with everything that he has come in contact with—including medicine.; A brilliant student who will not have to be given a place in the world—he'll make one for himself.

name: Wilkes, Claude C.
motto: "Yet have I not well begun; Battles so bravely won?"
description: An energetic straight-forward lad who was a real asset to Ole Miss, and whom we shall ever miss.

name: Cooper, Robert W.
motto: "A man who lives in his profession."
clubs: Student Assistant in Histology and Anatomy
description: A man who did well the work at hand, perhaps not a Jack-of-all-Trades, yet a master of that which he wished to be.

name: Duncan, Georgia
motto: "Her voice was ever soft, Gentle, and low; an excellent thing in woman."
description: A popular co-ed from town. It was ever a pleasure to see her tread the campus's beaten paths.

name: Eckles, Wm. G.
motto: "How doth the busy bee?"
description: Truly a busy fellow is he, and one who thoroughly performs any task assigned him.

name: Fulmer, Herman K.
motto: "Men of real merit find favor everywhere, such is he."
description: Though quiet and unassuming he was an economic problem—he burned quantities of the midnight oil.

name: Hagaman, Robert Lee Jr.
motto: "I've done the deed, Now let me go."
clubs: Recording Secretary Y. M. C. A. '20-21
description: "Hagy" got his degree in order that he might be able to choose an ambition. We hope he'll succeed. He labored earnestly for Ole Miss, and we can only say that fortunate will be the community where he chooses to spend the rest of his earthly existence.

name: Harper, H. A.
motto: "Hope! thou nurse of young desire."
description: Let it be said of him, "He was a good boy." We all liked H. A. and we're awful sorry he's not going to be with us next year.

name: Hathorn, J. C.
motto: "At dawn across the ignorant hills he flashed his counsel wise."
description: "Happy" caught for Ole Miss on the S. I. A. A. championship team. Dependable, and a hard worker, success is within his grasp.

name: Hathorn, Lois E.
motto: "Smooth runs the water where the brook is deep."
description: Exceeding wise, fair-spoken, and persuasive, one who accomplishes that which she desires.

name: Howorth, M. B.
motto: "O you chorus of indolent reviewers, Look, I come to the test!"
description: Howorth was at his best in the classroom. Who ever knew him to be unprepared on a lesson? He is a true disciple of work.

name: Hume, Myra
motto: "She was a scholar, and a ripe and good one; Exceeding wise, fair spoken, and persuading."
description: Wisdom lurked within her stately brow and sometimes came forth to survey the region round about. 'Twas then we heard things strangely wise.

name: Lipscomb, Hubert S.
motto: "And not in utter nakedness, But in trailing clouds of glory do I come."
description: When "Lip" first came among us we all took a look at his profile and decided that he was a man of super-mental abilities. Our decision has been more than verified.

name: Langston, J. D.
motto: "Wisdom is oft concealed in mean attire."
description: As large in mind as in body. A fine chap, always happy, ever ready to please others, one who holds fast his place in the hearts of his friends.

name: Lester, Drane
motto: "He walked with winning grace From easy effort to success."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Cabinet and Council
Vice President Y. M. C. A. '21
University Representative M. I. O. A. '18-'19-'20
One of University's Candidates for Rhodes Scholarship
description: Drane has an excellent reputation in the classroom, in athletics, and in student activities. You never found him in the rear ranks of any meritorious cause.

name: McLarty, Wallace Howd
motto: "See how the world its veterans reward."; "Much more a noble and right generous mind to virtuous moods inclined."
description: Not a "Beau Brummel", yet a "ladies man." "War Hoss" was quite an orator and parliamentarian.; Did you ever hear "War Hoss" lead a yell? If he becomes as efficient a doctor as he has been Cheer Leader, he'll be a howling success!

name: Kramer, W. A.
motto: "He holds fast the golden mean And lives contented, therein."
description: One man who does not have to stay here four years to be remembered.

name: Thompson, McClellan B. S.
motto: "One who never turns his back, but marches breast forward."
clubs: National Committeeman Ole Miss Chapter Alpha Phi Epsilon
Y. M. C. A. Council and Cabinet
Secretary Y. M. C. A. Cabinet 1919-'20
description: He had a voracious appetite for student activities, and it is said he never suffered indigestion from over-indulgence.

name: Lavinia, McLaurin B. A.
motto: "And yet a spirit still and bright With something of an angel light."
description: One whose poise and self-possession mark her as an outstanding character. She has the happy faculty of always saying something when she speaks.

name: John, Riley Mcdaniel Jr. M. D.
motto: "Why should I worry when there is no cause?"
description: From the outside seemed to be a don't care sort of fellow; but there was more to him than you suspected.

name: Francis, Pratt Mcgahey B. S.
motto: "Just wait until his ship comes sailing in!"
description: He was at home anywhere you put him. A good student and a loyal friend he was liked by all who knew him.

name: Nicholson, Mary Irma
motto: "She dwelt among untrodden ways, Among the springs of Dove."
description: A good student and bound to lead in cultured ranks among whatever people she may deign to live. We hope she may make for herself a record in life equally as good as the one she has made here.

name: Zeller, Raymond Bryant
motto: "Maidens, Why should you worry whom you shall wed."
description: Since the day he got "spiculed" in biology laboratory we've had a sort of notion that Zeller is more or less eccentric. Whether or not you believe it he was a thinker.

name: Pickens, Horace Dever
motto: "Perseverance is the key to success."
description: He was from Pontotoc but did his best to overcome that handicap. Pickens was a tennis enthusiast, but could do other things well.

name: Potts, W. E.
motto: "By their works you shall know them."
clubs: Student Assistant in Business and Commerce
description: Here was a man worthy of confidence. He made few public speeches but you should hear him parlez-vous with the ladies.

name: Mattox, Granville W.
motto: "Along the cool sequestered vale of life He keeps the noiseless tenor of his way."
description: Jake was a philosopher of the first magnitude—if you could but fathom his meanings. Stay with it, Jake, stay with it.

name: Huston, James Robert
motto: "True as a needle to the pole, Or as a dial to the sun!"
description: "My B. S. Will it see me safe through my journey or leave me bogged in the mire?"

name: Hopkins, Sadie
motto: "And Wisdom's self Oft seeks to sweet retired solitude."
description: To know her was to recognize her worth. "Sis" still believes that youth is everlasting.

name: Mattox, Vesta
motto: "Books! 'tis dull and endless strife Come hear the woodland linnet. How sweet his music! On my life there's more wisdom in it!"
description: During her two years with us she has made many friends and learned the secret of making high grades with little effort. She conceals her thoughts as well as anybody and can express them better than many. She is a good talker as well as a good listener.

name: Phelps, Eva
motto: "Oh, blessed with temper whose unclouded ray, Can make tomorrow as cheerful as today."
description: Vivacious, full of life and pep and never too tired to lead any undertaking that is worth while. She is usually happy and sees the bright side of everything. We admire her for her frankness of opinion.

name: Hougland, Horace
motto: "Can build castles in the air."
description: Likability plus ability was Horace through and through.

name: Slough, Jonnie Ethelyn
motto: "And give me music, music, food for those that trade in love."
description: One to whom Nature has been lavish in her endowments. A girl universally admired, who has the faculty of making friends wherever she goes. Her artistic temperament dominates her actions and her personality stands out distinctly.

name: Tubb, Thomas Jefferson
motto: "I'd rather be the first man among these fellows than second in Rome."
description: "Every Tubb must stand on its own bottom." - Tubb. He has done this ever since he was a Freshman.

name: Scott, Robert W.
motto: "No one knows what he can do till he tries."
description: He came, he saw, he conquered.

name: Mauldin, Janice Willard
motto: "Faithful through endless confusion— Loyal and honest, sincere."
description: Diligent, conscientious, upright, she lets no opportunity pass unheeded. Her individuality shines out as do her accomplishments and in one accomplishment she excels—Mathematics.

name: Rogers, Paul
motto: "Tout bien ou rien."
description: Though slightly aesthetic in temperament and revelling in oriental dreams he is a lover of the best the world has to offer and a seeker after truth and wisdom.

name: Ramey, Lynda
motto: "Happy art thou, as if every day thou hadst picked up a horseshoe."
description: Being a citizen of Oxford we are indeed surprised at her wisdom. She was a remarkable student and a loyal supporter of "Ole Miss."

name: Rowsey, James Melvin
motto: "He did not beat back the current, Yet he was not drowned in its waters."
description: Rowsey worked well for Ole Miss, we're hoping he'll do as well, nay, better, in the great school of experience.

name: Russell, Earl Watkins
motto: "And master of himself though China fall."; "Oh blest with temper whose unclouded ray Can make tomorrow cheerful as today."
description: Another Mississippi College man who's outgrown his training. He is indeed one of the best-liked men on the campus.; Quite an optimist and one who left the worry to the other fellow.

name: Russell, Edward Lee
motto: "Excelsior".
clubs: Anniversary Speaker, Reporter, Chaplain Phi Sigma
description: A grasping mind and courage to do or say what he thinks regardless of the multitude. He looked for the right and not for popularity.

name: Scott, Arnold Henry
motto: "We will now discuss the experiment in a little more detail."
clubs: Delegate to Student Volunteer Convention, Des Moines '20
description: One who was a shark in the sciences. He did not follow the crowd when he thought that it was in the wrong.

name: Catchings, C. E.
motto: "A sudden thought strikes me,—let us swear an eternal friendship."
description: Let's all gather around and sing "For he's a jolly good fellow". Yes, that's "Charlie" Catchings.

name: Dickson, Nolan Stewart
motto: "Born for success he seemed, With grace to win, with heart to hold"
clubs: B. S. A. and M. College
description: "Dick" outgrew A. and M., aspired to higher things, and decided to get an Education.

name: Holehan, M. W.
motto: "Solid men make no long orations."
description: He needs no trumpet to advertise his merits, they speak for themselves.

name: McGavhey, T. P.
motto: "The hills of manhood wear a noble face."
description: Who ever saw the man that didn't like "Mac?" Well, he was always "doing things"—that was the reason.

name: Ramsey, Granville S.
motto: "Write me as one who loves his fellow-men."
description: In love, out of love and back again is what you will find "George" Ramsey.

name: Reid, W. L.
motto: "Of science and logic he chatters as fine and as fast as he can."
description: One of the steadiest men we knew. "Doc" had a habit of mastering things.

name: Leighton, W. Ray
motto: "Oh, with temper whose unclouded ray Can make tomorrow cheerful as today."
description: Jolly. Likable, good-for-nothing "Peter" Ray will carve on you and me and turkey with equal ease!

name: James, Shelby Shipman
motto: "Gie fools their silks and knaves their wine. A man's a man for a' that!"
description: A man known, loved, and liked by all. Success to him is a matter of course.

name: L., C. Henderson
motto: "Seas roll to waft me, suns to light me rise; My footstool earth, my canopy the skies."
description: A combination of Doctor and basketball star is to be respected—that's Louis.

name: McDaniel, J. R.
motto: "If I can't have a bushel of silver, I'll do with a barrel of gold."
description: Where "Peanuts" was, pessimism could not remain. He was inherently an optimist.

name: Wallace, J. T., Jr.
motto: "And he whistled at his work."
major: M. D., B. S. Mississippi College '17
description: Wallace whistles and works in equal quantities, and he does both constantly. He is about fifty per cent energy and about fifty per cent ability.

name: Lipsey, James Holline
motto: "He lives to build, not to boast."
major: M. D., B. S. Miss. College '21
description: A man who outlived his early training and became a real Ole Miss man.

name: McNease, B. Wilbur
motto: "His pensive cheek and pondering brow, Told more than he was wont to vow."
description: He was a fine lad, was "Mac." He could twirl a ball and also a girl if the music was good. And because of these talents we've always wondered why he studied medicine.

name: Amsler, Guy
motto: "I am lord of all I survey."
description: As District Attorney he earned the fear and admiration of the criminals of University County. He thinks he can do a thing whether he can or not.

name: Austin, Chester W.
motto: "I dare do all that may become a man. Who dares do more?"
description: If his height is indicative of his genius we shall expect to see him reach the utmost pinnacle of fame.

name: Williams, R. X.
motto: "No solemn sanctimonious face I pull!"
description: Robert X. had a heart as big as the world itself. When we won a game, his picture show was ours.

name: Brown, D. W.
motto: "True friendships laws are by this man express—"
major: LL. B., B. S., A. and M. College
description: Brown with his corn cob pipe and cheerful disposition was a good dispeller of the blues.

name: Currie, Braxton L.
motto: "Don't seem the sort of man, do I, as might be the pal of kings?"
clubs: Representative State Legislature from Leake County
description: Not a Switzer yet a yodler. Currie was a law-maker and a man of worth.

name: Curd, Havilah Price
motto: "Be gone my cases, I give you to the winds."
description: One who could play as carefree as any and yet you could find some seriousness.

name: Dinsmore, Robert R.
motto: "You shall pity the king on his throne, But never the man whose learning is his own."
description: We would like to hear Dinsmore plead a case before the J. P. of district five, we feel that he would affect the pine burrs as well as the jury.

name: Hindman Doxey, Ll. B.
motto: "Slave to no sect, who takes no private road, But looks through Nature up to Nature's God."

name: Colin O. Gilbert, Ll. B.
motto: "The greatest men are the silent men."
description: Reserved and thoughtful with a heart as big as his mind.

name: Charles D. Williams, Ll. B.
motto: "The reward of a thing well done is to have done it."
clubs: B. A. and M. A. University of Virginia '17
description: Truly a disciple of thoroughness. If his success as a lawyer depends upon knowledge his success is assured.

name: Ellis C. Henry, Ll. B.
motto: "I must have liberty Withal as large a charter as the wind, To blow on whom I please."
description: This lad hails from regions not unknown. He was not center on the football team but he was the nucleus of his law class.

name: William, Haralson
motto: "I thank whatever gods may be for my unconquerable soul."
description: "Pinkie" was a capable and gritty worker and a real thinker. The opposing counsel had better beware or they may lose their laurels.

name: Patrick, Henry, Jr.
motto: "Give me liberty or give me death!"
description: When he thinks, he thinks thoughts; when he does, he does things.

name: Henry, Ware Hobbs
motto: "Great of heart and strong of mind."
description: We will hear from Hobbs in after years. As a student he was modest and in his breast there resided the soul of a true man.

name: H., M. Hood
motto: "The strangest combination of brain and brawn."
description: The man who was noted for his "million yard forward passes". Here's hoping that he has equal success with his first case!

name: Jourdan, J. C.
motto: "He had a power; In his eye There was a quenchless energy."
description: Jim was one of the silent leaders of the student body. He gained little fame as an orator but his advice on weighty questions was worth listening to.

name: Mccall, John Edward
motto: "I'm forever blowing bubbles!"
description: Many of the boys were disappointed when the student body elected someone else as the biggest liar. Take him all in all "Mac" is Prince of Good-fellows.

name: Mars, Adam L.
motto: "But in his motion like an angel sings, Still quiring to the young-eyed cherubims."
description: Quiet and unassuming, we would like to know his thoughts. He has a full appreciation of the beautiful and the aesthetic.

name: Mcelroy, Taylor Holcomb
motto: "An experienced, ambitious, and often quite picturesque man."
description: He was known as the "Eternal Freshman." He had a cool head and quiet mind.

name: Morton, P. W.
motto: "He thought as a sage but felt as a man."
description: Pete had naught but defiance and scorn for the world's worst chance. He tells great tales of wondrous experiences in Haiti, and of feminine conquest in the U. S.

name: Myers, Lewis A.
motto: "Courtliness and the desire of fame, And love of truth and all that makes a man."
description: He has a well-defined purpose in life. His splendid ability forces one to recognize Myers.

name: Sykes, J. B.
motto: "Eat, drink, and be merry for tomorrow we die."
description: Why all this sorrow? Let joy be unconfined, so says Red Sykes.

name: West, Clifton A.
motto: "He wanders as free as the winds of the mountains, Save Love's willing fetters."
description: Does he love the ladies or his books most? Who can tell? Well anyway, he is strong in both.

name: Scott, A. S.
motto: "Fire in each eye, papers in each hand, he roves, recites, and maddens 'round the land."
description: Scott gained a reputation as maker of "nice distinctions." Have you ever heard him sing? Well, er—ah—he was a student as well as an entertainer.

name: Stockett, Peter McKenzie
motto: "In eye and on lips his high confidence smiled!"
description: Pete with the pep. That's what he was called. Did you ever hear him throw the— Oh well!

name: Wasson, Ben F. Jr.
motto: "From grave to gay, from lively to severe."
description: A lover of the artistic, but not lacking in practicality.

name: Wells, J. M.
motto: "As big in soul as in body."
description: "Senator" says, "nobody loves a fat man," but he is not altogether right for we love to hear him laugh. His motto was, "Pass the skids."

name: Butler, Alton Chester
motto: "Ornament of a meek and quiet spirit."
description: Quiet sort of chap, but a fast friend and a good student. He will ever be a credit to himself.

name: Caffey, E. N.
motto: "Whose armor is his honest thought, And simple truth his utmost skill."
description: He goes forth to bless the world and at the same time to show it that he was made for success.

name: Cary, Emily Myrtle
motto: "If there's delight in love 'tis when I see That heart, which others bleed for, bleed for me."
clubs: Executive Committee Ole Miss Pharmaceutical Association
description: One whom the graces have smiled upon. Lovable by nature and studious by inclination, she is entitled to an opinion of her own and does not mind using it against an adversary.

name: Carleton, E. E.
motto: "Happy am I; from care I'm free!"
description: Happy and care-free is Carleton through and through. Gloom fades from his presence.

name: Jones, Otto Briggs
motto: "Nothing in the extreme, but all in the degree."
description: One of the youngest members of his class and a favorite with all.

name: Land, Milton Leland
motto: "Singing he was, or whistling, all the day."
description: "Minnie" came to us from Mississippi College and immediately assumed an enviable place in our hearts. His bright, happy manner was irresistible.

name: Miller, Edmund F.
motto: "Whatever sceptic could inquire for, For every why he had a wherefore."
description: What's the use of work anyway, things come by luck!

name: Pearson, Edgar Aaron
motto: "He walks no more where shadows are, But left their ivory gates ajar."
description: He was a prince of a chap, and we cannot but predict success for him in the calling he has chosen.

name: Pettis, W. E.
motto: "When taken, To be well shaken."
description: "For every ill, I've got a pill", is his motto.

name: Purvis, T. H.
motto: "The victories of Right Are born of strife."
description: In two years he has stepped into a "four-year" place in our hearts.

name: Riddell, T. H.
motto: "Haste, for our hearts are with thee, take no rest!"
description: A pill-roller who uses his art in baseball. Not his fault that he can be from no better place than Kilmichael; but his friends must needs think that he hails from the land of the wise.

name: Schwartz, John E.
motto: "I'm not a man of the quitin' kind."
description: "Schlitz with the grit" sticks to a job until it's finished and then—he sits down.

name: Scott, R. Newman
motto: "Pleasure and action make the hours seem short."
description: Remember how Scott made things hum in the American Legion Minstrel, last year? Well, he's just the humming kind!

name: Sharron, Daniel B.
motto: "Beware the fury of a patient man."
description: "Thank you, call again! Pills are my specialty!" Oh well, who knows but that he will own half interest in Van Vleet-Mansfield some day!

name: Tennant, William L.
motto: "Just bent on playing the game."
description: Care-free, happy-go-lucky "Tom" Tennant. An eighteen karat disciple of the Great Gawd Luck!

name: Warner, James B.
motto: "Be swift to hear, Slow to speak, Slow to wrath."
description: Good at anything that he turns his hand to. In the future he will be a "pill-roller" of the A-1 Class.

name: Wells, Ira Marvin
motto: "Tomorrow comes and we are where? Then let us live today."
description: And one man wanted a castle, another a beautiful wife, a third would cruise in a palace yacht like a red-necked prince of blood, but Wells wants naught but a Ph. G.

name: Stewart, H. R.
motto: "I am not a politician, and my other habits are good."
description: "Hosea Perlinsky" has had the reputation of conducting the most attractive clothing swindle on the campus.
